name: Belgian Waffles with Honey
steps: Whisk the flour, egg, and milk into a smooth batter.
Cook in a hot, lightly oiled waffle iron until golden.
Drizzle with the honey.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, egg, milk, melted butter, and baking powder into a smooth, pourable batter.
Cook in a hot, lightly oiled waffle iron, 3-4 minutes, until deeply golden and crisp at the edges.
description: Drizzle generously with the honey - Belgian waffles, especially the yeasted Liege-style version, are a genuine point of national pride, sold fresh from street stalls across the country.

name: Belgian Eggs with Cheese
steps: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter.
Fold in the cheese and pepper just before they finish setting.
steps_detailed: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter over low heat, stirring often for soft curds.
Fold in the cheese and pepper just before they finish setting, letting it melt through.
description: A simple egg and cheese breakfast.

name: Belgian Oat Porridge
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top generously with the honey.
description: A simple breakfast porridge.

name: Belgian Moules-Frites (Mussels and Potatoes)
steps: Toss the potato wedges with a little oil and roast at 425F for 20-25 minutes, turning once.
Soften the onion in the butter, add the mussels and a splash of water, cover, and steam 6-8 minutes until they open.
Serve together, discarding any mussels that don't open.
steps_detailed: Toss the potato wedges with the oil and roast at 425F (220C) for 22-28 minutes, turning once, until crisp and golden.
Soften the onion and garlic in the butter, 5-6 minutes, add the mussels and three-quarters cup water, cover, and steam 7-9 minutes until the shells open.
description: Serve together, discarding any mussels that stayed closed - moules-frites, mussels steamed simply and served alongside crisp fries, is Belgium's most iconic dish.

name: Belgian Stoemp (Mashed Potato with Vegetables)
steps: Simmer the cabbage until tender, about 12-15 minutes.
Mash the potato with the milk and butter, then mash the cabbage through it.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cabbage until fully tender, 14-16 minutes, then drain.
Mash the potato with the milk, butter, and pepper until smooth, then mash the cabbage through it.
Stoemp, mashed potato worked through with a vegetable, closely mirrors Dutch stamppot, reflecting the shared Low Countries culinary tradition.
description: Stoemp, mashed potato with vegetables folded through, a Belgian comfort food staple.

name: Belgian Chickpea Stew

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Soften the onion in the butter.
Add the chickpeas and tomatoes, and simmer 15-18 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Soften the onion and garlic in the butter, 5-6 minutes.
Add the chickpeas and tomatoes, and simmer 18-22 minutes, mashing some of the chickpeas to thicken.
description: A simple chickpea stew.

name: Belgian Cheese and Ham-Style Toast

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the bread and layer with the cheese.
Warm the milk with a little more cheese into a light sauce, pour over, and broil 3-4 minutes until bubbling.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread and layer with half the cheese.
Melt a little butter, stir in the flour, then the milk, simmering until thickened, and stir in the remaining cheese until melted.
Pour the cheese sauce over the bread and broil 4-5 minutes until bubbling and golden - croque monsieur-style melts are a common lunch across Belgium, closely tied to its French-speaking south.
description: A simple cheese and ham toast.

name: Belgian Beef Stew (Carbonnade-Style)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion, cooking the onion until deeply caramelized, 12-15 minutes.
Stir in the flour and honey, add 1 cup water, cover, and simmer 40-45 minutes until very tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, butter, and pepper over medium-high heat, then cook the onion low and slow, 14-17 minutes, until deeply caramelized.
Stir in the flour and honey, add 1.25 cups water, cover, and simmer 42-48 minutes until very tender and the sauce has thickened into a rich, dark gravy.
description: Carbonnade flamande is traditionally braised in dark Belgian beer - a touch of honey stands in here for the malty sweetness that beer would otherwise provide.

name: Belgian Cheese and Potato Bake
steps: Layer the potato with the milk in a small dish and bake at 375F, covered, for 30 minutes.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 10-15 more minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the potato with the milk, butter, and pepper in a small baking dish, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 32-35 minutes until tender.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 12-15 more minutes until golden and bubbling.
description: A simple cheese and potato bake.

name: Belgian Baked Salmon with Butter
steps: Season the salmon with the pepper and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until just cooked through. Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Season the salmon with the pepper and dill and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 16-20 minutes until just cooked through and flaking easily.
Finish with the lime juice.
description: A simple baked salmon with butter.

name: Belgian Chocolate-Dipped Fruit

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Melt the chocolate.
Dip the apple slices in the chocolate and scatter with the walnuts.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Melt the chocolate with the butter until smooth and glossy.
description: Dip the apple slices halfway into the chocolate and scatter with the walnuts, then chill briefly until set - Belgian chocolate, prized worldwide, is treated with real reverence at home too, not just in the famous pralines shops.

name: Belgian Bitterballen-Style Fried Beef Balls
steps: Form the beef into small balls and simmer until cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, and fry in the oil until golden.
steps_detailed: Form the beef, seasoned with the pepper, into small balls and simmer in water 9-11 minutes until cooked through.

Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, and fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes, until deeply golden and crisp - bitterballen are shared between Belgium and the Netherlands as a beloved bar snack.
description: Bitterballen, deep-fried breaded meat ragout balls, a beloved Belgian and Dutch bar snack.

name: Belgian Cheese and Waffle Snack
steps: Whisk the flour, cheese, egg, and milk into a batter.
Cook in a hot, lightly oiled waffle iron until golden.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, cheese, egg, milk, and pepper into a smooth batter.
Cook in a hot, lightly oiled waffle iron, 3-4 minutes, until golden and the cheese has melted through - a savory spin on the classic Belgian waffle.
description: A simple cheese and waffle snack.

name: Belgian Mussel Snack
steps: Steam the mussels with the garlic and butter, covered, 6-8 minutes until they open.
Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion and garlic in the butter, 3-4 minutes.
Add the mussels and a splash of water, cover, and steam 7-9 minutes until the shells open.
Finish with the lime juice, discarding any mussels that stayed closed.
description: Mussels, central to Belgian cuisine, served here simply.

name: Belgian Chocolate and Walnut Snack
steps: Melt the chocolate and toss with the walnuts and honey, then chill until set.
steps_detailed: Melt the chocolate with the butter until smooth, toss with the walnuts and honey, and spread in a small lined dish to chill and set.
description: Belgian chocolate paired simply with walnuts.

name: Belgian Chocolate Mousse
steps: Melt the chocolate with the milk.
Whisk in the egg and honey off the heat, chill until set.
steps_detailed: Melt the chocolate with the butter over low heat until smooth. Let cool slightly.
Whisk the egg yolks with half the sugar until pale and thick, then whisk into the cooled melted chocolate.
In a separate, completely clean bowl, whip the egg whites with the remaining sugar until they hold stiff, glossy peaks.
Fold the whipped whites into the chocolate mixture in two or three additions, folding gently to keep the air in rather than stirring it out.
Chill at least 2 hours until set - real mousse should feel light and airy, not dense.
Note: this uses raw egg whites and yolks, which carry a small salmonella risk - pasteurized eggs are a safer choice if serving to anyone pregnant, elderly, very young, or immunocompromised.
description: Mousse means foam - real aeration comes from whipping egg whites separately and folding them in, not just whisking a whole egg into melted chocolate. Belgian chocolate's quality carries real depth even in a home version.

name: Belgian Waffle Cookies
steps: Cream the butter and honey, mix in the flour and cinnamon, and shape into small rounds.
Bake at 350F for 12-15 minutes until lightly golden.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter, honey, and cinnamon until light, then mix in the flour and a little egg until it forms a soft dough.
Shape into small rounds and bake at 350F (175C) for 13-16 minutes until lightly golden - speculoos-style spiced cookies are a beloved Belgian classic served with coffee.
description: Simple waffle-style cookies.

name: Belgian Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
description: A simple rice pudding.

name: Belgian Mustard Sauce
steps: Warm the mustard, butter, and milk together gently until smooth.
steps_detailed: Warm the mustard, butter, milk, and pepper together gently over low heat, stirring, until smooth.
Good with fries or grilled meat.
description: A simple mustard sauce.

name: Belgian Chocolate Sauce
steps: Melt the chocolate with the milk and honey over low heat, stirring, until smooth.
steps_detailed: Melt the chocolate with the milk, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring constantly, until smooth and glossy.
Good over waffles, ice cream, or fruit.
description: A simple chocolate sauce, Belgian chocolate being world-renowned for its quality.
